frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: history biases toward or away from previously encoded stimuli

frameworks.name: multivariate decoding of working memory
frameworks.one_line: MEG classifiers read out orientation representations during encoding

frameworks.name: sensory vs postperceptual loci
frameworks.one_line: tests whether history bias arises in encoding or in later memory/decision stages

core_question: Do serial-dependence biases on working-memory reports arise during sensory encoding or at postperceptual stages?
key_assumptions: orientation representations are decodable from MEG during encoding
behavioral biases can be decomposed into within-trial and between-trial components
neural representational bias and behavioral bias can be compared on the same scale
manipulations: two sequentially presented gratings with one retro-cued for recall
comparison of within-trial prior orientation vs between-trial prior orientation
key_findings: behavior showed a within-trial repulsive bias away from the other same-trial orientation
behavior showed a between-trial attractive bias toward the previous trial's cued orientation
MEG-decoded encoding representations were repelled from the prior orientation in both within- and between-trial cases
neural repulsion occurred regardless of opposite behavioral bias direction
interpretations: repulsive history effects emerge at the level of sensory processing
postperceptual maintenance or decision stages can override sensory repulsion to yield attractive behavioral biases
